tagline: Impute missing counterfactual outcomes by exploiting low-rank structure in the treated-unit-by-time outcome matrix.
selection_problem: The naive comparison E[Y|D=1] - E[Y|D=0] conflates treatment effects with pre-existing differences between treated and control units. Selection bias equals E[Y_i(0)|D=1] - E[Y_i(0)|D=0], which is nonzero whenever treated units would have had different trajectories even absent treatment.
counterfactual_problem: We never observe Y_i(0) for treated units post-treatment; the matrix of untreated potential outcomes has a block of missing entries precisely where treatment occurs.
po_setup: Let Y_i(1) be unit i outcome under treatment and Y_i(0) under control; D_it = 1 if unit i is treated in period t. The estimand is ATT = E[Y_it(1) - Y_it(0) | D_it=1], averaged over treated unit-time cells.

estimand_math.label: Assumption
estimand_math.expr: E[Y_it(0)] = L_it where rank(L) = r << min(N,T)

po_intuition: The PO framework makes explicit that the entire post-treatment block of Y_i(0) for treated units is unobserved data to be imputed, not a modeling choice. Regression obscures that DiD and synthetic control are special cases of filling in this missing block under different rank constraints.
solution: Matrix completion exploits the fact that the N×T matrix of untreated potential outcomes is approximately low-rank, using observed control units and pre-treatment periods of treated units jointly to impute the missing block. Control units and pre-treatment treated outcomes together pin down the latent factors.
key_insight: DiD assumes rank-1 with additive unit and time effects; synthetic control assumes T factors; matrix completion relaxes both by letting the data determine the rank via nuclear norm regularization.

assumptions.name: Low-Rank Potential Outcomes
assumptions.intuition: If untreated outcomes are driven by many unrelated factors, the matrix is not compressible and imputation fails.

assumptions.name: Missing Completely At Random (or STAG)
assumptions.formal: Treatment assignment is independent of idiosyncratic errors given L
assumptions.intuition: If which cells are treated depends on the unit-time shocks, the observed entries are not representative and imputation is biased.

example_setup: A retail chain rolls out a loyalty program in 30 of 100 stores starting in 2020; the other 70 stores never adopt it. Weekly revenue per store is observed 2017-2022, and stores that adopted were generally higher-revenue before treatment.
example_counterfactual: We cannot observe what weekly revenue the 30 treated stores would have earned in 2020-2022 had they never received the loyalty program.
example_execution: Matrix completion fills the missing 30-store × 3-year block by factoring the observed 100×5 pre-treatment matrix plus the 70-control × 3-year post block into low-rank components. The imputed values serve as Y_i(0) for the treated stores post-2020.
example_result: The ATT estimate is +$4,200 per store per week, meaning treated stores earned $4,200 more under the loyalty program than their imputed no-treatment counterfactual.
source_title: Matrix Completion Methods for Causal Panel Data Models

math_panel.label: Low-rank model
math_panel.expr: Y_it(0) = L_it + ε_it, L = U V' with rank r

math_panel.label: Nuclear norm
math_panel.expr: min over L: sum_{obs} (Y_it - L_it)^2 + λ ||L||_*

math_panel.label: ATT estimate
math_panel.note: Observed minus imputed for treated cells

did_shape.caption: Matrix completion imputes the counterfactual trajectory (dashed) for treated stores using low-rank structure; the gap to observed outcomes is the ATT.
